title: Celebrating Beautiful Differences Together
story: People with albinism have little or no pigment in their skin, hair, and eyes, making them look wonderfully unique! On June 13, International Albinism Awareness Day helps everyone learn about and celebrate these amazing differences. Schools share stories about famous people with albinism, communities host educational events, and social media fills with supportive messages. This special day fights myths and bullying while showing that being different is something to be proud of, not hide from.
activity_question: What makes people with albinism look different from others?
activity_choices: They have little or no pigment in their skin, hair, and eyes
They are much taller than average
They can only see in black and white
They have extra fingers and toes
activity_answer: They have little or no pigment in their skin, hair, and eyes
follow_up_question: How can you help make someone who looks different feel welcome and included?
